definition: having the ability to use your senses to see and to feel
example: Many ethicists argue that sentient animals deserve special moral consideration.

definition: relating to or characterized by excluding something or someone; narrow in scope
example: The committee adopted an exclusionary policy that left several groups out of the discussion.

definition: choose the safer or more cautious option when uncertain
example: When in doubt about safety, engineers err on the side of caution.

definition: a feeling of deep respect or awe
example: She showed reverence for the ancient ritual and observed it quietly.

definition: the relationship between members of the same family or a feeling of close connection
example: They felt an immediate kinship despite having just met.

definition: an expert who studies and forecasts the weather
example: The meteorologist predicted rain throughout the weekend.

definition: understanding of a situation only after it has happened
example: In hindsight, we should have started the project earlier.

definition: an attitude of doubt or questioning about a claim or belief
example: Public scepticism grew after the first contradictory report was published.

definition: is a sign that something good will happen in the future
example: The early positive reviews bode well for the movie's box office.

definition: a general agreement among a group of people
example: After hours of discussion they reached a consensus on the plan.

definition: very loyal and committed in attitude
example: She is a staunch supporter of the environmental movement.

definition: the quality of being extremely thorough, careful, and precise
example: The study was praised for its scientific rigour.

definition: whether an entity deserves moral consideration
example: Philosophers debate the moral status of nonhuman animals.

definition: the belief that humans are fundamentally different from or superior to other species
example: Human exceptionalism has shaped many historical attitudes toward nature.

definition: a method in ethics of balancing principles and judgments to arrive at coherent beliefs
example: They adjusted their rules through a process of reflective equilibrium.

definition: nerve receptors that detect damaging stimuli (pain)
example: The presence of nociceptors suggests the animal can sense potentially damaging stimuli.

definition: a person who commits a crime or harmful act; culprit
example: Police identified the perpetrator from surveillance footage.

definition: (n) a boat that carries people or vehicles across water; (v) to carry by ferry
example: They took the ferry across the river to the island.

definition: able to be clearly seen, touched, or measured; concrete
example: The benefits of the program were tangible and immediate.

definition: a short period of intense activity, illness, or effort; also an instance (e.g., a bout of flu)
example: She suffered a brief bout of the flu and recovered quickly.

definition: a wonderful or astonishing thing; something that inspires wonder
example: The ancient cathedral was a marvel of medieval engineering.

definition: the study of religion and religious beliefs
example: He studied theology at university to better understand religious traditions.

definition: impressive in size, appearance, or manner
example: The castle made an imposing sight on the hill.

definition: to force something (a rule, duty, or burden) on someone
example: The new authority tried to impose strict curfews on the city.

definition: to cause someone to suffer something unpleasant (pain, harm, loss)
example: The storm inflicted significant damage on the coastal town.

definition: (1) an approximate calculation; (2) a time when actions are judged or accounted for
example: The final reckoning revealed how much money had been spent.

definition: (1) to think or suppose; (2) to calculate approximately
example: I reckon we'll arrive by noon if traffic stays light.

definition: to transform sediment or other material into rock; to harden into stone
example: Over millions of years, sediments lithify into sedimentary rock layers.

definition: having many small, complex parts or details; complicated
example: The clock's mechanism is intricate and requires expert repair.

definition: waste material expelled from the body; fecal matter
example: Scientists analyzed animal excrements to study diet and health.

definition: the state of being important, well-known, or easily noticeable; visibility or fame
example: Her research gained prominence after the award announcement.

definition: to dig in the ground to uncover buried objects or structures
example: Archaeologists excavate ancient sites to learn about past civilizations.

definition: extremely small; tiny
example: The error was minuscule and did not affect the final result.

definition: to gradually reduce the size, number, or amount of something
example: They whittled down the guest list to only close family.

definition: to cause severe destruction, damage, or emotional shock
example: The hurricane devastated coastal towns and displaced thousands.

definition: the quality of occurring in cycles; periodic fluctuations
example: Economic cyclicality affects hiring and investment decisions.

definition: with great care, attention, and persistence; diligently
example: She worked assiduously to complete the thesis on time.

definition: the arrangement of natural and artificial physical features of an area (rivers, mountains, etc.)
example: The topography of the region made road construction difficult.

definition: able to be perceived, noticed, or understood; noticeable
example: There was a discernible improvement in his language skills after the course.

definition: a firm warning or reprimand
example: The teacher's admonition reminded students to submit assignments on time.

definition: abundant; in large amounts
example: She took copious notes during the lecture.

definition: a channel or tube through which something (water, information, electricity) passes
example: The old pipeline served as a conduit for fresh water.

definition: to give up, let go of, or stop doing something; to surrender control or possession
example: He decided to relinquish his role as project lead.

definition: to enjoy thoroughly or take great pleasure in
example: She would relish the chance to work abroad again.

definition: an unlikely stroke of luck; a chance occurrence
example: Their victory was a fluke, but they celebrated it nonetheless.

definition: completely lacking or free from something
example: The desert was devoid of vegetation after the long drought.

definition: nutrient overload in water bodies leading to algal blooms and oxygen loss
example: Eutrophication from fertilizer runoff damaged the lake's ecosystem.

definition: rapid growths of algae or seaweed that can harm ecosystems
example: Algal blooms reduced oxygen levels and killed many fish in the bay.

definition: an ocean process that brings nutrient-rich deep water to the surface, supporting high productivity
example: Coastal upwelling zones are often rich fishing grounds.

definition: recycling organic waste into a soil conditioner (compost) or the process of doing so
example: Composting kitchen scraps enriches garden soil and reduces waste.

definition: promoting equal rights, opportunities, or access for all people
example: They advocated for egalitarian policies in education and employment.

definition: caused or produced by human activity
example: Many environmental problems are anthropogenic in origin.

definition: divided and assigned shares according to a plan or proportion; allocated fairly
example: The budget was apportioned among departments based on need.
